mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in real retail sales signals consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly RSAFS changes do not coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent signals extreme monetary accommodation that reduces short-term discount rates and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observed weeks over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous monthly decline in industrial production and increase in consumer prices compress corporate profit margins through higher input costs and lower output volumes, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of INDPRO monthly decline and CPIAUCSL monthly increase fails to produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly changes in payroll employment predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent, because hiring gains signal accelerating economic recovery.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, positive PAYEMS changes do not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 850 thousand units indicate robust residential investment that supports construction and materials sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 850 fail to precede positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage point signal deteriorating labor income and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month UNRATE increases exceeding 0.2 percentage point do not produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth does not positively predict S&P 500 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad-based demand-supply expansion that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint monthly increases in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.
